skill.name: test-coverage-advisor
skill.description: Reviews Rust test coverage and suggests missing success, error, edge case, async, and organization tests.
skill.summary: Find missing Rust test cases and improve coverage strategy.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. Markdown code fences were flagged as Ruby backticks, ordinary text such as description was flagged as weak cryptography, and example test strings were flagged as reconnaissance. The skill is documentation-only and declares Read, Grep, and Glob tools, with no command execution, network access, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior found.

content.value_statement: Rust projects often miss error paths, boundaries, and async failure cases. This skill reviews tests and suggests practical coverage improvements for stronger code quality.
actual_capabilities: Identifies missing success, error, and edge case tests in Rust code.
Suggests async test coverage patterns for Tokio-based functions.
Recommends table-driven tests when similar cases are repeated.
Reviews test organization for success, error, and edge case modules.
Flags weak tests that lack assertions or check implementation details.
limitations: Focused on Rust test strategy, not general application testing across all languages.
Does not run coverage tools or execute test suites by itself.
Requires project code or test files for precise recommendations.
Cannot guarantee full coverage without human review of business rules.

use_cases.title: Review New Rust Features
use_cases.description: Check whether a new implementation has success, failure, boundary, and async tests before review.

use_cases.title: Improve Pull Request Quality
use_cases.description: Find missing tests in a pull request and request focused additions before merge.

use_cases.title: Strengthen Test Suites
use_cases.description: Identify repeated testing gaps and improve team standards for Rust coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single Rust function.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust function and its tests. List missing success, error, and edge case tests in priority order.

prompt_templates.title: Review Error Paths
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused check for Result and Option handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Rust tests for uncovered error paths. Suggest specific cases for each Result or Option branch.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Test Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Organizing a growing module test suite.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust test module. Suggest clearer organization, table-driven cases, and assertions that improve maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Coverage Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Rust feature for coverage gaps. Include business logic risks, async behavior, boundaries, and regression test priorities.

output_examples.input: A Rust validator has tests for valid input but no invalid input tests.
output: Add tests for empty input, malformed input, and missing required fields.
Assert the exact error variant when the public API exposes one.
Keep success and error tests separated so failures are easy to diagnose.

output_examples.input: A module has many similar validation tests with repeated setup.
output: Replace repeated cases with a table-driven structure.
Give each case a clear description so failures explain the scenario.
Keep one focused assertion goal per test group.

output_examples.input: An async repository method only tests the found-record path.
output: Add tests for missing records, backend errors, and empty identifiers.
Use the project async test runtime consistently.
Verify observable behavior instead of internal repository state.

best_practices: Provide the implementation and existing tests together for accurate coverage review.
Ask for prioritized gaps when time is limited before a release.
Turn suggested cases into project-specific tests with exact error expectations.
anti_patterns: Do not treat coverage percentage as proof that important behavior is tested.
Do not add broad tests that hide several behaviors behind one failure.
Do not rely on generated suggestions without checking domain rules.

faq.question: Does this skill run my tests?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and suggests missing tests. It does not execute commands or collect coverage data.

faq.question: Which language does it support best?
faq.answer: It is written for Rust projects and uses Rust testing patterns throughout its guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with async tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests async success and failure cases for functions that use async runtimes such as Tokio.

faq.question: Can it improve existing tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest clearer assertions, table-driven cases, and better module organization.

faq.question: Does it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It highlights likely gaps, but a reviewer should confirm business rules and risk priorities.

faq.question: Why was the security risk marked safe?
faq.answer: The flagged patterns are documentation examples, not active execution. The skill only requests read and search tools.
